Troy was my state senator for a number of years when I lived in his district.

A really big thing people need to know is that his district is not a traditional district. Northern Maine has had a strong Democratic presence because of the French Catholic population which was heavily discriminated against by protestants and historically the states Republicans.

And mind you, when the KKK came to Northern Maine, they were largely made up of Republicans and focused on targeting Catholics.

This means that the Democratic Party within this part of Maine has a really strong and more stable position, partly because it is hyper focused on local issues with a continual legacy of representation to back it up.

Troy is able to do well in this district I think in part because there is still a deep connection to the Democratic Party in that area that I do not fully know, resonates with other deeply red areas and other parts of the country where the Democratic Party has been unplugged from being seen as anything but mirroring the policies of the national Democrats. It’s a place that will vote for Trump vote for a Democrat for state level politics because of that complicated history where the Democrats can win election elections because they’re still a local knowledge of them being able to address local issues in a way that cuts through the narratives that they are just the national Democratic Party. If that makes sense.

Maybe that’s a good thing, maybe this will actually work. But I do want to caution people that just because the area voted for Trump means that it is more reflective of Troy than the unique political history of that area.

news.title: Platner ally Troy Jackson locks down delegates as top rivals for Democratic Senate nomination drop out
news.description: Troy Jackson locked up delegates after rivals Shenna Bellows and Nirav Shah dropped out of the race to replace Graham Platner against Susan Collins.

news.title: Troy Jackson appears headed for Maine Democratic nomination in race against Susan Collins
news.description: The logger and former Maine Senate president appeared to have dominated Saturday's delegate-selection meetings in half of Maine's counties. His top rivals for the nomination had withdrawn from the race or suspended their campaigns by Sunday evening.

news.title: Jordan Wood drops out of Senate race, endorses Troy Jackson
Troy Jackson pledges to continue progressive 'movement' as he closes in on Senate nomination
news.description: The logger and former Maine Senate president turned out supporters across the state over the weekend, especially among Maine's organized labor community and former supporters of Graham Platner.

Troy Jackson pledges to continue progressive 'movement' as he closes in on Senate nomination
news.description: The logger and former Maine Senate president turned out supporters across the state over the weekend, especially among Maine's organized labor community and former supporters of Graham Platner.

news.title: Troy Jackson dominates Maine Democratic caucuses, emerges as frontrunner to face Susan Collins
